sn36.4:1.0.1: "Bhikkhus, when the uninstructed worldling makes the statement, ‘In the great ocean there is a bottomless abyss,’he makes such a statement about something that is nonexistent and unreal. This, bhikkhus, is rather a designation for painful bodily feelings, that is, ‘bottomless abyss.’
sn36.4:1.0.2: One who cannot endureThe arisen painful feelings,Bodily feelings that sap one's life,Who trembles when they touch him,
sn36.4:1.0.3: A weakling of little strengthWho weeps out loud and wails:He has not risen up in the bottomless abyss,Nor has he even gained a foothold.sn.iv.207
sn36.4:1.0.4: But one who is able to endure them—The arisen painful feelings,Bodily feelings that sap one's life—Who trembles not when they touch him:He has risen up in the bottomless abyss,And he has also gained a foothold.
